text: Debugging is an essential skill for any programmer. When you identify a bug, it’s important to take a systematic approach instead of guessing. Start by understanding the expected behaviour of your code and then check where it differs. Using debugging tools like breakpoints or step-through debuggers can help you examine variables and flow. Another useful strategy is adding logging statements to track what your program is doing at specific points. By following a clear and structured process, you can fix issues more efficiently and write more reliable software.
